applied.negative_prompt: text, watermark, logo, ui overlay, readable interface text, warning labels, document text, code text, poster design, collage fragments, gore, injury close-up, patient suffering, named victim, sensational burn imagery, crowded scene, many small objects, unreadable background detail, motion blur, action pose, blockbuster cinematic lighting, multiple surreal events, competing bright accents
applied.positive_prompt: source-preserving documentary image, one clear subject or scene, subject remains recognizable after simplification, one dominant mechanism-bearing tension or contradiction, controlled palette, restrained lighting, clear silhouette, low clutter, non-spectacular tension, vertical 9:16 frame, A concrete system-safety mechanism composite anchored to a Therac-like treatment unit and turntable path: one control cable or software-mediated channel appears to carry both command and safety responsibility, while the expected independent physical interlock is absent as an empty bracket or disconnected mechanical position. Preserve machine-room and turntable logic; reject code-only framing. One dominant absence: no separate safety layer between the wrong state and the beam path. muted industrial neutrals, clinical documentary lighting, no warning signs, no labels, no readable UI.

notes: Derived from the official ComfyUI Flux.2 Dev text-to-image workflow.
Adapted to expose negative_prompt, batch_size, seed, and filename_prefix for this lab.
The negative prompt branch is retained as an unconnected CLIPTextEncode node because the official FLUX.2 Dev text-to-image path is positive-guidance-first.
Export-time model loader filenames are patched from FluxLab runtime config so local-safe checkpoints can replace the official quantized default.
